title: Option Care Health Appoints Robert Okunski as Vice President, Investor Relations
author: Na
description: Option Care Health, the nation's largest independent provider of home and alternate site infusion services, announced the appointment of Robert Okunski as Vice President of Investor Relations effective May 26, 2026. Okunski brings over two decades of investor relations experience across healthcare, technology and energy sectors, and will lead the company's investor relations strategy and stakeholder communications.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The appointment of an experienced investor relations executive with a strong track record signals the company's commitment to enhancing investor engagement, transparency, and corporate visibility. This is a positive development for shareholder communication and demonstrates management's focus on executing growth strategy and creating long-term shareholder value.

title: OPCH SHAREHOLDER ALERT: Investors Encouraged to Contact Kirby McInerney LLP About Potential Securities Laws Violations
author: Kirby Mcinerney Llp
description: Law firm Kirby McInerney LLP is investigating Option Care Health for potential securities law violations following a significant stock price decline. The company issued optimistic guidance in February 2026 but missed Q1 earnings expectations in April, causing shares to drop 24%. The firm is seeking investors who purchased OPCH securities to discuss potential claims.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company missed Q1 2026 earnings consensus estimates, reduced full-year revenue guidance, experienced a 24% stock price decline, and is under investigation for potential securities law violations. Management's confident guidance statement in February proved inaccurate, raising concerns about disclosure practices.

title: Levi & Korsinsky Announces Investigation of Securities Claims Against Option Care Health (OPCH)
author: Levi & Korsinsky, Llp
description: Option Care Health (OPCH) shares fell approximately 26-30% after the company reported a Q1 2026 revenue miss and slashed full-year 2026 guidance by $125-225 million, down from $5.8-6.0 billion to $5.675-5.775 billion. The significant guidance cut came just two months after management reaffirmed the original guidance on the Q4 2025 earnings call. Levi & Korsinsky has initiated a securities fraud investigation examining whether the company made materially false or misleading statements regarding patient census changes and Stelara patient conversions.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock declined 26-30% following Q1 2026 revenue miss and substantial downward revision of FY2026 guidance ($125-225 million reduction). Management's recent reaffirmation of guidance two months prior raises concerns about potential misstatement of material facts regarding patient census and drug conversion assumptions.

title: GE HealthCare Technologies Inc. Shareholders Are Encouraged to Reach Out to Johnson Fistel for More Information About Potentially Recovering Their Losses
author: Johnson Fistel, Pllp
description: Johnson Fistel, PLLP announced investigations into GE HealthCare Technologies, Option Care Health, and Rocky Brands on behalf of investors who suffered losses. GE HealthCare disclosed significantly reduced earnings guidance and a $250 million increase in expected input costs due to inflation, leading to a sharp stock price decline.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company reported declining earnings per share, reduced full-year guidance, and disclosed unexpected $250 million in additional input costs due to inflation, resulting in sharp stock price decline and triggering securities investigation.
Subject of securities investigation by Johnson Fistel on behalf of investors who suffered losses, indicating potential legal and financial issues.
Subject of securities investigation by Johnson Fistel on behalf of investors who suffered losses, indicating potential legal and financial issues.

title: Option Care Health, Inc. Investors Should Contact Block & Leviton to Find Out How They Might Recover Money Through The Firm’s Investigation
author: Block & Leviton
description: Option Care Health's stock fell over 30% on April 30, 2026, after the company reported weaker-than-expected first quarter results and cut full year revenue guidance due to a larger than anticipated patient census reset in its chronic inflammatory disease portfolio. Block & Leviton is investigating potential securities law violations and encouraging affected investors to contact them.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock fell 30% following disappointing Q1 results and reduced full-year revenue guidance. The company's patient census assumptions proved significantly worse than expected, indicating operational challenges and potential prior misrepresentation of guidance.
